notes:
Diamond & Pearl base set (dp1), 2007 — first set of the DP era. Booster pack = 10 cards (increased from 9 in the prior era), confirmed by Bulbapedia: 5 Common, 3 Uncommon, 1 Reverse Holofoil, 1 Rare-or-Rare-Holo. Box = 36 packs. No ETB (vintage 2007 set predates Elite Trainer Boxes, ~2013).

RARE SLOT (1 per pack): contains regular rare, rare-holo, or Level X. Level X rate = 1:36 packs (0.0278) per Flipside Gaming's rarity review, which explicitly states "approximately 1:36 packs for the first sets in the Diamond and Pearl era" — dp1 is the first set, so this is the directly-applicable figure (≈1 Level X per booster box). The remaining 0.9722 of the rare slot is split between the 25 regular rares and 17 rare-holos proportionally to their pool sizes (no per-set holo:rare study exists for dp1, so pool-proportional mapping is used): rare = 0.9722×25/42 = 0.5787, rare-holo = 0.9722×17/42 = 0.3935.

REVERSE SLOT (1 per pack): DP-era reverse-holo parallels existed for commons, uncommons, and regular rares (holo-rares and Lv.X had no reverse parallel). Distributed by pool size across the 110 eligible cards: common 47/110=0.4273, uncommon 38/110=0.3455, rare 25/110=0.2273.

CONFIDENCE = low: no large published per-set pull-rate study for dp1; figures rest on an era-level Level X rate (1:36, well documented and consistent across sources) plus pool-proportional mapping for the holo/rare and reverse splits. Vintage booster boxes carry a large sealed/collector premium, so EV/booster will sit well below box market price — expected, not a flaw. PokeBeach pull-rate thread (page 17) returned HTTP 403 and could not be fetched; cited as a corroborating source surfaced in search but not independently verified.
